title: OPF Chart Preset
description: Schema for chart-preset records in the pptx.gallery library. Each record describes a single named chart variant — the OOXML/Aspose chart type, its series/category cardinality, the column structure of the underlying workbook, and a small sample dataset suitable for previews. Chart presets are referenced from OPF chart content payloads; the engine resolves the reference against catalogs.charts (inline) → catalogs.charts.source → the default catalog at https://www.pptx.gallery/charts.

$schema.description: Identifies this record as a chart preset in the pptx.dev library.

id.description: Stable slug used by OPF documents to reference this preset. Lowercase kebab-case. Chart preset ids may start with a digit (e.g., '100pct-stacked-column', '3d-column') — unlike most other catalog ids — to mirror conventional chart-naming.

name.description: Internal name (often UPPER_SNAKE_CASE matching the engine's enum).

label.description: Human-readable label shown in chart pickers.

summary.description: One-sentence positioning — when to reach for this chart variant.
examples: Single-series vertical column chart for comparing values across categories.
Bullet chart that benchmarks one value against ranked threshold bands.

description.description: Longer prose describing the chart and ideal use cases.
examples: Use a clustered column when you want to compare a small number of series across the same categories — e.g., revenue by quarter for two product lines. Best with 2–4 series and ≤12 categories.

type.description: Engine chart-type identifier (Aspose / OOXML form). Distinct from an OPF chart content payload's discriminator 'type' — this is the catalog record's own engine-level chart kind.

group.description: Top-level grouping in the chart picker (column, bar, line, area, pie, radar, etc.).

groupSort.description: Display ordering hint within the chart group.

complexity.description: Shape of the underlying data: a flat series ('simple'), one with engine-side calculation ('calculated'), parent-child rows ('hierarchical'), or pre-normalized rows ('normalized').

series.description: Number of data series this preset expects.

categories.description: Number of category labels this preset expects on the primary axis.

seriesGroups.description: Number of series groups (axis bands) this preset uses; >1 for combo or banded charts.

useSecondaryCategories.description: Whether the preset uses a secondary category axis.

workbookRange.description: A1 reference to the source range in the embedded workbook.

columns.description: Column header names of the embedded workbook, in left-to-right order.

dataColumns.description: Per-column metadata describing the role and position of each column in the workbook source.

helperColumns.description: Optional auxiliary column names used by calculated or banded charts (e.g., 'Excellent', 'Good', 'Fair', 'Poor' for a bullet chart).

sampleData.description: Inline sample dataset for previews and pickers.

slideNumber.description: Source slide number in the original chart-gallery deck. Carried for traceability.

tags.description: Free-form labels for filtering and search.

preview.description: Visual previews of the record, used by picker UIs and inline rendering. All sub-fields are optional; engines fall back gracefully when previews aren't available.

src.description: Main preview image (PNG/JPG). Used as the primary visual in picker UIs and previews of the record.

thumbnailSrc.description: Smaller thumbnail preview suited to dense grid views.

vectorSrc.description: SVG / vector preview for crisp scaling at any size.

ChartDataColumn.description: One column of the embedded chart workbook, annotated with its role and grid position.

role.description: Role this column plays: a category label (axis tick), a series (plotted values), or a helper (calculated/auxiliary).

type.description: Cell value type for the column.

position.description: Grid position of the column header in the source workbook, as 'row<N>_col<M>' (zero-indexed).

ChartSampleData.description: Inline sample dataset for previews. Mirrors a small workbook with header row plus data rows.

headers.description: Header row labels. The first cell typically labels the series column; the rest are category labels.

rows.description: Two-dimensional sample data. Each row aligns by index with the headers — first cell is the row label, remaining cells are values.
